title: Destroying Then Rebuilding Data
concept: Diffusion deliberately destroys structure through noise, then a learned model rebuilds structure from that noise incrementally.

exercises.prompt: If you scribble random noise all over a photo until it's unrecognizable, could you ever get the photo back?

options.text: No, once it's noisy it's gone forever
Yes, if something learned exactly how the noise was added, it could learn to undo it step by step
Only if you redraw it by hand from memory

exercises.explain: Surprisingly, if you know exactly HOW noise was added, you can train a model to reverse that process a tiny bit at a time — that's the big idea behind diffusion!

cards.title: From Static to a Masterpiece
cards.body: Imagine watching TV static slowly morph into a crystal-clear photo. That's not magic — it's diffusion! AI models like this start with pure random noise and, step by step, sculpt it into a brand new image, sound, or pattern.

cards.title: What Is Diffusion?
cards.body: Diffusion is a two-part trick. First, you take real data (like a picture) and slowly add random noise until it becomes pure static — this is called the 'forward' process. Then, you train a model to reverse this: starting from noise, it removes a little bit of noise at a time until a new, realistic piece of data appears.

cards.title: Melting and Refreezing Ice
cards.body: Think of a detailed ice sculpture slowly melting into a puddle — that's destroying structure. Diffusion is like learning to run that video backward: watching how a puddle could refreeze, drop by drop, into a brand-new sculpture, even one that never existed before!

cards.title: Painting From Static
cards.body: An AI art generator starts with a canvas of random colored fuzz. Over dozens of tiny steps, it nudges the fuzz — removing a little noise each time — based on your text prompt 'a cat wearing a crown.' After enough steps, a clear, detailed image of that royal cat appears.

cards.title: Myth: It Just Cleans One Blurry Photo
cards.body: Diffusion isn't like a photo-editing 'sharpen' filter that fixes one blurry pic. It builds something completely NEW from scratch by denoising random static, guided by everything it learned from millions of images during training.

cards.title: Two Directions, One Model
cards.body: Diffusion has a forward path (destroy) and a reverse path (rebuild). The model only needs to learn the reverse path well — undoing noise step by step to create something new.

left.text: Real image → add noise again and again → pure random static

right.text: Pure random static → remove noise again and again → new realistic image

exercises.prompt: What does a diffusion model learn to do?

options.text: Reverse a noise-adding process, step by step, to build new data
Sort images into folders by category
Translate text from one language to another

exercises.explain: Diffusion models learn the reverse process: starting from random noise and gradually denoising it into realistic new data.

exercises.prompt: In diffusion, the model rebuilds an image in one single instant step, not gradually.
exercises.explain: False! Diffusion rebuilds data incrementally, removing a small amount of noise at each of many steps until the final result emerges.

exercises.sentence: Diffusion models first learn how noise ___ a picture, then learn to reverse that process to create new pictures.
exercises.explain: The 'forward' process destroys structure by adding noise; the model then learns to reverse this destruction.

exercises.prompt: Which best describes the starting point when a diffusion model generates a brand-new image?

options.text: A low-resolution copy of a real photo

exercises.explain: Diffusion generation begins with random noise, which the model gradually denoises into a coherent, realistic image.

exercises.prompt: A friend says: 'Diffusion models just blur and unblur one existing photo to make it look nicer.' What's wrong with this claim, and what's the better description?

options.text: Nothing is wrong — that's exactly what diffusion does
Diffusion trains by learning to reverse noise added to real data, then generates brand-new data by denoising random static from scratch — it's not just touching up one existing photo
Diffusion only works on text, never on images
Diffusion instantly creates images with no steps involved

exercises.explain: Diffusion models learn from many examples how noise destroys structure, then use that reverse skill to build entirely new outputs step by step, starting from random static — not editing one existing photo.
